title: Carbeth Plants - Herb Plants - Grow Your Own Herb Garden - Lavender angustifolia Plant in 14cm Pot - Lavandula English Lavender Bush
features: SUMMER COLOUR - Lavender are popular, aromatic, drought-tolerant garden plants. They are easy to grow and associate well with other shrubs, perennial plants and roses. It produces masses of fragrant purple/blue flowers during the Summer on wiry spikes above silver-grey or grey-green foliage. This lavender plant can be used for cooking in the kitchen due to its amazing flavours.

PLANTING POSITION - All lavender must have good drainage and close to full sun. They prefer poor soil and will thrive in exposed coastal sites. When established, they are pretty much totally drought-tolerant, but in the first and second year you should water them as you would any other new shrub to make sure they establish well.
HEIGHT AND SPREAD - This plant will grow to a height of about 0.5-1 metres with a spread of 0.5-1 metres.
WHAT YOU WILL RECEIVE - This plant will arrive in a 14cm growing pot.

Grown right in your garden or kitchen, they add authentic aroma and flavour to any dish. Easy to Grow Herb plants thrive with minimal care. Whether on a sunny windowsill or patio pot, they provide continuous harvests through the seasons. Sustainable Growing your own herbs reduces plastic packaging and food waste. Simply harvest what you need right at home. Attracts Pollinators Herbs not only look and smell wonderful, they attract bees and pollinators, supporting a healthy, vibrant garden ecosystem. Can herb plants grow outdoors in the UK? Yes, many herbs like rosemary, thyme, chives and mint grow well outdoors in the UK. Tender herbs like basil prefer warmer months or indoor conditions. Are herb plants easy to grow for beginners? Yes, herbs are low maintenance and great for beginners. They need light, occasional watering, and trimming to thrive. How soon can I harvest herbs after planting? Herbs grow quickly. You can start harvesting leaves within a few weeks once the plant is established and leafy. Will herbs regrow after cutting? Yes, most herbs will regrow after cutting. Regular trimming encourages fuller growth and better flavour. Will herb plants survive winter? Hardy herbs like thyme and rosemary can overwinter outdoors. Tender types like basil should be brought inside.
